brief_summary: Time-axis sonic schematic for the H2 'Why the feel survives'. Top-of-travel anchor at left, bottom-out at right. Two events: a sharp bordeaux-emphasized spike at the top of travel ('dome collapse — sharp, audible, tactile') with a focal dot, followed by a dashed near-linear travel segment ('linear travel, progressive spring'), then a broad rounded crest with a bordeaux wash at the right end ('bottom-out — damped by dome flattening'). Caption strip: 'two acoustic events on a Topre press; only the first is sharp'.
data_sources: topre-electrocapacitive-deep-dive.mdx § 'Why the feel survives' — 'The dome collapses near the top of the press, well before the spring has compressed enough to actuate, and the collapse itself is sharper than the bump on a Boba U4T ... After the dome collapses, the remaining travel is essentially linear ... The bottom-out itself is then damped by the rubber dome ... There are two acoustic events on a Topre press and only one of them is sharp.'
